task objective: Implement Syncthing activity status strip monitoring
design decisions: Resolved import of updater at the top level in service.py and shortened delegation/facade methods to stay within the 400-line class size limit.
Integrated Syncthing activity monitoring into pre-game and post-game status strip flows.
Decoupled startup checks to prevent slow Syncthing Flatpak/native API responses from blocking game launch or game exit.
Utilized a session token inside gameLifecycleController.tsx to isolate poll callbacks and prevent older Syncthing watches from leaking or overriding newer watches.
Stopped the Syncthing watcher before presenting launch-gate conflict resolution modals, resuming it only after a resolution is selected.

Excluded pending_remote_ack from update_in_progress to avoid showing active upload/download messages during idle sync, but retained it in settled checks to defer the final checkmark transition until peers acknowledge the sequence.
results: All Python and TypeScript unit tests passed successfully.
Code formatting and type checks passed (ruff, ty, pnpm run verify).
Successfully verified and packaged the plugin ZIP, with Codex review gate reporting no discrete, actionable correctness issues.
